note: Auditable view-coverage decision table for the sit-and-reach 2x4 FRAME variant (task SITFRAME). Each candidate area is scored on (1) installation/fabrication complexity and (2) difficulty of seeing it from the primary whole-model views, then decided ZOOM or a recorded WHY-NOT. Audited BOTH directions. Renders: scripts/render_sitframe_views.py -> outputs/sit_reach_frame/views/.
context_treatment: The floor is a role:existing context body sized to the sitter zone (the ply variant's V2 lesson applied from the start). The frame is OPEN — unlike the closed ply box most joinery reads from outside; the rail-corner zoom still hides the -X side so the +X joints read unobstructed (x-ray convention, ply-box V1 lesson).

primary_views.shows: whole frame on the floor from the sitter's side — 2x4 legs + ply top, 23cm overhang
THE protocol elevation: 12in reach surface + the 23cm foot-line overhang past the foot plane
the two footplate legs at stance width, rail end grain flush at the top, open center
the reach/scale surface and the four cap-screw stations

candidate_areas.area: whole frame form (four legs, two full-depth rails, capping ply top)
candidate_areas.rationale: the frame reads as one mass over the context slab in the primaries; the floor-hidden iso is where the leg/rail/plate construction reads, so it leads the document.

candidate_areas.area: rail-to-leg cleat corners (the frame's only lateral joinery) + plate-on-rail bearing
candidate_areas.rationale: highest local density — two cleat_screwed joints per rail AND the separately-declared plate-on-rail bearing, all tucked under the overhanging plate; only an inside angle with the far side hidden makes the rail band, its screw heads, and the plate seat read. The hidden -X corners' screws float in frame (x-ray convention, caption says so).
mirror-identical to the +X corners about the Y-Z plane; one representative zoom suffices.

candidate_areas.area: footplate legs as the foot bearing (the variant's design idea)
candidate_areas.view: z_foot.png (floor hidden, from the sitter's side)
candidate_areas.rationale: the FRONT primary shows the strips head-on but flattens depth; the sitter's-side zoom under the overhang is where 'the legs are the footplates' reads as a three-dimensional fact — full-height strips, open center, plate overhead.

identical metrology to the ply variant, and this frame's open form leaves it fully visible in BOTH the side primary and z_foot; a third view of the same cantilever would be over-generation. The callouts dimension it.

candidate_areas.area: plate -> leg-top rail_cap joints (screwed into end grain)
candidate_areas.rationale: four screw stations read in the TOP primary; the seat itself (plate on leg end) is a flush plane the connections + hardware-presence table prove. The ply box's z_corner precedent showed one representative screw-into-edge; here the same joint class reads in z_rail's plate seat.

candidate_areas.area: governing protocol dims (12in height, 23cm foot line)
candidate_areas.rationale: dimensioned by the derived callouts on the SIDE primary + asserted with LITERAL expecteds in-model + guarded by the e2e test.
four flush bearing planes, no fabrication; primaries show the legs standing; the proof is in the findings table.

candidate_areas.area: scale/gradation zone on the top plate
candidate_areas.rationale: a finishing step, not modeled geometry (same disclosure as the ply variant); nothing fabricated to draw.

no section/cutaway in this render path; presence is VALIDATED (hardware-presence PASS + allowlisted overlaps) and z_rail's x-ray shows the -X corners' screws in open frame. An honest render limitation.
